pending.body: finding_summary: The returned `mock_br` fixture is only usable for one hard-coded command sequence. `build_mock_br_adapter()` preloads a single FIFO queue of responses, and `MockBrAdapter::run()` blindly pops the next entry, so any integration test that calls `show` before `list`, repeats a query, or skips one of the smoke-test steps will receive the wrong payload or exhaust the mock. That makes the advertised reusable scenario fixture unreliable outside the one embedded smoke test. References: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:344-359`, `src/test_support/br.rs:154-184`. remediation_hint: Make the scenario mock dispatch by command/arguments (or maintain command-aware mutable state) so `list`/`show`/`create`/`close`/`sync` work in any reasonable order.

finding_summary: The mocked `br show` payloads do not actually match the bead graph because `br_show_json()` hard-codes `dependents` to an empty array. In this scenario, the root epic should report downstream dependents and the prepare task should report the validate task as a dependent, but any code that consumes `BeadDetail.dependents` will see an empty graph. That can hide regressions in downstream-context and planned-elsewhere logic that rely on reverse edges. References: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:427-453`, `src/cli/project.rs:377-390`, `src/cli/project.rs:1778-1835`. remediation_hint: Derive `dependents` by scanning the scenario issues for reverse dependencies when constructing each `show` response.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 5 -> Plan and Implement
